channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans plugged away overnight and into early Saturday morning to approve their multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts framework, hurtling past Democratic opposition toward what President Donald Trump calls the “big, beautiful bill” that's central to his agend
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, LEAH ASKARINAM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: President Donald Trump's new tariffs mark an all-in bet that his economic vision will pay off for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio teacher attempted to pay student $2K to kill estranged husband in failed murder-for-hire plot
item.description: An Ohio substitute teacher is accused of offering a student $2,000 to kill her estranged husband before the teenager's mother discovered the plan and alerted authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP pushes Trump budget framework through after marathon vote series
item.description: An amended version of the House-passed Trump budget was voted on at the end of a marathon vote series in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: The federal government wants the number of offices that oversee U.S. mine safety laws to align more with a shrinking coal industry.
item.dc:creator: John Raby And Leah Willingham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: IRS plans to cut up to 25% of staff, starting with closing its civil rights office, AP sources say
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The IRS plans to cut as many as 20,000 staffers — up to 25% of the workforce — as part of layoffs that began Friday, two people familiar with the situation told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump administration to cut teacher-training money, for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday granted the Trump administration’s plea to cut hundreds of millions of dollars in teacher-training money as part of its anti-DEI efforts, while a lawsuit continues.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington and Oregon sue over Trump elections order, saying mail voters could be disenfranchised
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington and Oregon on Friday became the latest states to ask a court to reject President Donald Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections in the U.S., a day after Democratic officials in 19 others filed a similar lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Liberation Day' Backstory
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling agency that funds community groups in Latin American countries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed on Friday to block the Trump administration from dismantling an independent agency that distributes grant money to community development groups in Latin American and Caribbean countries. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese-owned GNC stores operating on US military bases spark national security concerns
item.description: Rep. Pat Harrigan warns GNC’s Chinese ownership poses a national security threat as the company operates 85 stores on U.S. military bases without disclosing ownership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Budget begins to take shape as leaders huddle
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with the top budget adviser to the governor: Erin Campbell, commissioner of Minnesota Management and Budget. Then, a conversation with the new DFL Party Chair Richard Carlbom.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Thune faces GOP divisions on critical budget resolution
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) faces a number of divisions among Senate Republicans that could derail the Senate budget resolution, a measure that will be critical to passing President Trump’s legislative agenda later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing the “witch hunt” against her is yet another instance of critics in Europe using “lawfare” to quash free speech and censor political opposition.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fed chair: Tariffs could hike inflation, but more details needed
item.description: Powell's public appearance comes as Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs continue to rock economic markets worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: A Pentagon inspector general announces investigation into Hegseth’s use of Signal
item.description: The announcement comes after lawmakers voiced concern about whether top national security officials discussed classified information on Signal about a military strike against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Michigan couple Paul, Christy Akeo freed from Mexico prison
item.description: Paul and Christy Akeo had been arrested in Cancún on March 4 after a high-end resort company accused them of fraud.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coincidence? Recession-era artists releasing new music
item.description: "Recession pop" refers to the type of dance-floor jams that were popular around 2008 when the U.S. economy imploded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Social media platforms could be in for a popularity tax in Minnesota
item.description: A DFL bill would tax social media companies that access Minnesotans data. The lawmaker behind it said it could help bridge the state’s rocky budget outlook without adding costs for Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After federal firings, officials worry about program that helps many Minnesotans pay for heat
item.description: Minnesota was scheduled to receive an additional $12 million to $13 million in LIHEAP funds to help another 10,000 households. If that payment is delayed, the program could run out of money by mid-April.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: After promising transparency, RFK guts public records teams at HHS
item.description: Despite promises for "radical transparency," Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. laid off many staff on teams that fulfill public records requests at health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s tariffs won’t give US workers ‘any pain’: Karoline Leavitt
item.description: Roughly 60 countries are being singled out for the reciprocal tariffs, the majority of which are set at half of the tariff those countries place on U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: E-Verify legislation starts to move at the statehouse, passing its first committee
item.description: The state legislature is now at the halfway point in the 2025 legislative session and as the end swiftly approaches, lawmakers in Tallahassee are pushing for more immigration reform.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Tuesday’s elections, including bad news for Elon Musk
item.description: Democrats can take yesterday’s elections as one of the first bits of good news they’ve had in months. But there are lots of issues that are still center-right, including immigration.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9
